title: Invoking failure to reject ASCII-only Punycode-encoded labels in golang.org/x/net/idna
snippet: The ToASCII and ToUnicode functions incorrectly accept Punycode-encoded labels that decode to an ASCII-only label. For example, ToUnicode("xn--example-.com") incorrectly returns the name "example.com"

title: Invoking incorrect handling of namespaced elements in foreign content in golang.org/x/net/html

title: Invoking denial of service when parsing arbitrary HTML in golang.org/x/net/html
snippet: Parsing arbitrary HTML can consume excessive CPU time, possibly leading to denial of service.

title: Invoking infinite loop on large channel writes in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
snippet: When writing data larger than 4GB in a single Write call on an SSH channel, an integer overflow in the internal payload size calculation caused the write loop to spin indefinitely, sending empty packe

title: Invoking pathological RSA/DSA parameters may cause DoS in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
snippet: The RSA and DSA public key parsers did not enforce size limits on key parameters. A crafted public key with an excessively large modulus or DSA parameter could cause several minutes of CPU consumption

title: Invoking VerifiedPublicKeyCallback permissions skip enforcement in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
snippet: Previously, CVE-2024-45337 fixed an authorization bypass for misused ssh server configurations; if any other type of callback is passed other than public key, then the source-address validation would

title: Invoking auth bypass via unenforced @revoked status in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/knownhosts
snippet: Previously, a revoked 'SignatureKey' belonging to a CA was not correctly checked for revocation. Now, both the 'key' and 'key.SignatureKey' are checked for @revoked.
